name: Global Deterioration Scale
description: A clinician-rated scale for assessment of primary degenerative dementia, staging cognitive decline from normal aging through severe dementia across 7 stages.
instructions: Based on clinical interview and observation, select the stage that best characterizes the patient's current level of cognitive and functional decline.

questions.question: Select the stage that best characterizes the patient's current cognitive and functional status

options.label: Stage 1 - No Cognitive Decline
options.description: Patient appears normal clinically. No complaints of memory deficit. No memory deficit evident on clinical interview.

options.label: Stage 2 - Very Mild Cognitive Decline
options.description: Patient complains of memory deficit (forgetting where they placed familiar objects, forgetting names). No objective evidence of memory deficit on clinical interview. No deficits in employment or social situations. Appropriate concern about symptoms.

options.label: Stage 3 - Mild Cognitive Decline
options.description: Earliest clear-cut deficits. Objective evidence of memory deficit on intensive interview. Concentration deficit may be evident. Decreased facility remembering names, retaining information from reading. Decreased performance in demanding employment and social situations. May get lost traveling to unfamiliar locations. Denial and mild-moderate anxiety often present.

options.label: Stage 4 - Moderate Cognitive Decline
options.description: Clear-cut deficit on careful clinical interview. Concentration deficit on serial subtractions, decreased knowledge of current events, deficit in memory of personal history, decreased ability to travel alone and manage finances. Orientation to time and person preserved. Denial dominant, flattening of affect and withdrawal observed.

options.label: Stage 5 - Moderately Severe Cognitive Decline
options.description: Patient can no longer survive without some assistance. Unable to recall major aspects of current life (address, phone number, names of close family members, school attended). Some disorientation to time or place. Difficulty counting backward. Retains knowledge of own name and generally spouse/children's names. No assistance needed for toileting/eating but may have difficulty choosing proper clothing.

options.label: Stage 6 - Severe Cognitive Decline
options.description: May occasionally forget spouse's name. Largely unaware of recent events. Sketchy knowledge of past. Unaware of surroundings, year, season. Difficulty counting from 10. Requires substantial assistance with ADLs. May become incontinent. Diurnal rhythm disturbed. Recalls own name. Personality and emotional changes: delusions, obsessive symptoms, anxiety, agitation, possible violent behavior.

options.label: Stage 7 - Very Severe Cognitive Decline
options.description: All verbal abilities lost, frequently no speech (only grunting). Incontinent of urine, requires assistance with toileting and eating. Loses psychomotor skills including ability to walk. Brain no longer able to tell body what to do. Generalized cortical and focal neurologic signs and symptoms frequently present.

severityBands.label: Subjective Cognitive Impairment
severityBands.description: Normal aging or very early dementia (forgetfulness phase).

interpretation.scoringMethod: Single stage selection (1-7). This is a staging instrument, not a summed score.

stageCorrelations.stage3: May correspond to Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI)

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The GDS staging corresponds broadly to MMSE scores: Stage 4 ≈ MMSE 20-25, Stage 5 ≈ MMSE 14-19, Stage 6 ≈ MMSE 5-13, Stage 7 ≈ MMSE 0-4. Duration at each stage is highly variable.

references.citation: Reisberg B, Ferris SH, de Leon MJ, Crook T. The Global Deterioration Scale for assessment of primary degenerative dementia. Am J Psychiatry. 1982;139(9):1136-1139.

metadata.author: Reisberg B, Ferris SH, de Leon MJ, Crook T
